translations.idiomatic: The duke could not find him and set the hill afire; Jie Zitui refused to emerge and died in the flames.

sentences.zh: 」事具耿恭傳。

translations.literal: " The affair in full is in the biography of Geng Gong.
translations.idiomatic: The fuller account appears in the biography of Geng Gong.

sentences.zh: 龍，星，木之位也，春見東方。

translations.literal: Long is a star; it is the position of wood; in spring it appears in the east.
translations.idiomatic: The 'dragon' asterism belongs to the east and wood and rises in spring.

sentences.zh: 心為大火，懼火之盛，故為之禁火。

translations.literal: The heart is the great fire; fearing the excess of fire, therefore they made the prohibition against fire.
translations.idiomatic: The heart constellation is the Great Fire—people feared its heat and banned cooking.

sentences.zh: 俗傳雲子推以此日被焚而禁火。

translations.literal: Common tradition says Zitui on this day was burned and therefore fire was prohibited.
translations.idiomatic: Folk belief tied the taboo to the anniversary of Jie Zitui's burning.

sentences.zh: 注[二]其事見桓譚新論及汝南先賢傳也。

translations.literal: Note [2] The affair is seen in Huan Tan's New Discourses and the Biographies of Former Worthies of Runan.
translations.idiomatic: Note [2]: see Huan Tan's Xin lun and the Runan worthies' lives.

sentences.zh: 轉冀州刺史。

translations.literal: He was transferred to Governor of Ji Province.
translations.idiomatic: He was transferred to the governorship of Ji Province.

sentences.zh: 陽嘉三年，司隸校尉左雄薦舉，征拜尚書。

translations.literal: In the third year of Yangjia, Metropolitan Superintendent Zuo Xiong recommended Ju; he was summoned and appointed Master of Writing.
translations.idiomatic: In Yangjia 3 Metropolitan Superintendent Zuo Xiong recommended him; he was summoned to the Masters of Writing.

sentences.zh: 舉與僕射黃瓊同心輔政，名重朝廷，左右憚之。

translations.literal: Ju together with Vice Director Huang Qiong with one mind assisted in government; his name was weighty at court, and those to his left and right feared him.
translations.idiomatic: He and Vice Director Huang Qiong ran the administration in concert and were so formidable that the palace faction tread softly.

sentences.zh: 是歲河南、三輔大旱，五穀□傷，天子親自露坐德陽殿東廂請雨，又下司隸、河南禱祀河神、名山、大澤。

translations.literal: That year Henan and the Three Adjuncts had great drought; the five grains were [damaged]; the Son of Heaven personally sat in the open at the east wing of Virtue-and-Clarity Hall to pray for rain, and also ordered the Metropolitan Superintendent and Henan to pray and sacrifice to the River Lord, famous mountains, and great marshes.
translations.idiomatic: That year Henan and the capital region withered in drought; the emperor exposed himself in the east loggia of the Deyang Hall to beg rain and sent the metropolitan command and Henan to sacrifice to the river god and great peaks.

sentences.zh: 詔書以舉才學優深，特下策問曰：「朕以不德，仰承三統，[一]夙興夜寐，思協大中。

translations.literal: By edict, because Ju's talent and learning were excellent and deep, a special policy question was issued, saying: "We, lacking virtue, look up to inherit the three systems; [1] we rise early and retire late, thinking to harmonize with the great mean.
translations.idiomatic: An edict singled out Zhou Ju's erudition for a palace examination: 'I lack virtue yet inherit the triple mandate; [1] I rise early and sleep late, seeking the great mean.'

sentences.zh: [二]頃年以來，旱□屢應，稼穡焦枯，民食困乏。

translations.literal: [2] In recent years drought [lacuna] has repeatedly responded; the crops are burnt dry; the people's food is exhausted.
translations.idiomatic: [2] Yet droughts return year after year, the fields are charred, and the people go hungry.'

sentences.zh: 五品不訓，王澤未流，[三]腢司素餐，據非其位。

translations.literal: [3] The five grades are not instructed; the king's moisture has not flowed; the multitude of offices eat idle meals and occupy posts that are not theirs.
translations.idiomatic: [3] The five relationships go untaught, royal grace does not reach the people, and officials warm seats they do not deserve.'

sentences.zh: 審所貶黜，變復之征，厥□何由？

translations.literal: Examine those to be degraded and dismissed—by what route do the signs of restoration come?
translations.idiomatic: Whom should we dismiss to turn these omens around?'

sentences.zh: 分別具對，勿有所諱。

translations.literal: Distinguish and answer in full; do not have anything you shrink from."
translations.idiomatic: Answer plainly and hold nothing back.'"

sentences.zh: 」舉對曰：「臣聞易稱『天尊地卑，乾坤以定』。

translations.literal: " Ju replied, saying: "Your subject has heard that the Changes calls it 'Heaven honored, earth lowly—thereby Qian and Kun are fixed.'"
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Ju answered: 'The Book of Changes says, "Heaven is high, earth is low—thus Qian and Kun are fixed."'

sentences.zh: 二儀交構，乃生萬物，萬物之中，以人為貴。

translations.literal: The two powers intertwine, and thereupon produce the myriad things; among the myriad things, the human is most precious.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven and earth engender the myriad creatures, of which mankind is noblest.'

sentences.zh: 故聖人養之以君，成之以化，順四節之宜，適陰陽之和，使男女婚娶不過其時。

translations.literal: Therefore the sages nurture them with lords, complete them with transformation, comply with the fitness of the four seasons, suit the harmony of yin and yang, and cause men and women in marriage not to exceed the proper times.
translations.idiomatic: The sages therefore set rulers over them, shaped them through education, tuned life to the seasons and to yin-yang, and kept marriage within its proper age.'

sentences.zh: 包之以仁恩，導之以德教，示之以□異，訓之以嘉祥。

translations.literal: They wrap them in benevolent grace, lead them with virtue and instruction, show them [lacuna] anomalies, and train them with auspicious signs.
translations.idiomatic: They enfold the people in kindness, guide them with moral teaching, warn them with portents, and encourage them with good omens.'

sentences.zh: 此先聖承干養物之始也。

translations.literal: This is the beginning by which former sages received Heaven and nurtured things.
translations.idiomatic: Such is the way the ancient kings matched Heaven in caring for creation.'

sentences.zh: 夫陰陽閉隔，則二氣否塞；

translations.literal: As for when yin and yang are blocked and cut off, then the two qi are obstructed;
translations.idiomatic: When yin and yang are blocked,'

sentences.zh: 二氣否塞，則人物不昌；

translations.literal: when the two qi are obstructed, then things and people do not flourish;
translations.idiomatic: the two breaths choke each other,'

sentences.zh: 人物不昌，則風雨不時；

translations.literal: when things and people do not flourish, then wind and rain do not come in season;
translations.idiomatic: creatures fail to thrive,'

sentences.zh: 風雨不時，則水旱成□。

translations.literal: when wind and rain do not come in season, then flood and drought become [lacuna]."
translations.idiomatic: the seasons of wind and rain break down,'

sentences.zh: 陛下處唐虞之位，未行堯舜之政，近廢文帝、光武之法，而循亡秦奢侈之欲，內積怨女，外有曠夫。

translations.literal: Your Majesty occupies the position of Tang and Yu, yet has not enacted the government of Yao and Shun; you have recently cast aside the methods of Emperor Wen and Emperor Guangwu, and follow the desires of perished Qin's extravagance; within you accumulate resentful women, without you have husbandless men.
translations.idiomatic: and flood and drought follow.' Your Majesty sits where Yao and Yu sat yet does not rule like them; you have cast aside the ways of Emperors Wen and Guangwu for the extravagance of dead Qin—hoarding resentful concubines within the palace while common men lack wives.'

sentences.zh: 今皇嗣不興，東宮未立，傷和逆理，斷絕人倫之所致也。

translations.literal: Now the imperial heir does not flourish; the Eastern Palace is not yet established—it is brought about by injuring harmony and reversing principle, cutting off human relations.
translations.idiomatic: No heir has been named and the crown prince's quarters stand empty—this is what comes of violating harmony, inverting the natural order, and breaking human ties.'

sentences.zh: 非但陛下行此而已，豎宦之人，亦復虛以形埶，威侮良家，取女閉之，至有白首歿無配偶，逆於天心。

translations.literal: It is not only Your Majesty who practices this; the eunuch persons also again falsely rely on their power and situation, mightily insult good households, take women and shut them away, until there are those who reach white heads and die without mates—it goes against the mind of Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: Nor is the fault yours alone: eunuchs abuse their power, drag off women from decent families, and lock them in the palace until some die gray-haired and alone—Heaven cannot approve.'

sentences.zh: [四]昔武王入殷，出傾宮之女；

translations.literal: [4] In antiquity when King Wu entered Yin, he released the women of the Tilted Palace;"
translations.idiomatic: [4] When King Wu entered Yin he freed the harem of Shang.'

sentences.zh: [五]成湯遭□，以六事克己；

translations.literal: [5] When Cheng Tang met drought, by the six affairs he restrained himself;
translations.idiomatic: [5] When Tang faced drought he disciplined himself on six counts;'

sentences.zh: [六]魯僖遇旱，而自責祈雨：[七]皆以精誠轉禍為福。

translations.literal: [6] when Duke Xi of Lu met drought, he blamed himself and prayed for rain: [7] all by utmost sincerity turned disaster into blessing."
translations.idiomatic: [6] Duke Xi of Lu blamed himself and prayed for rain. [7] Sincerity turned calamity into blessing.'

sentences.zh: 自枯旱以來，彌歷年歲，未聞陛下改過之□，徒勞至尊暴露風塵，誠無益也。

translations.literal: From the time the drought withered the land until now, years have followed one another; I have not heard of Your Majesty's reforming faults [lacuna]; vainly toiling the most honored to expose himself to wind and dust truly has no benefit.
translations.idiomatic: The dry years have stacked up, yet no edict shows you mending what Heaven resents; parading the Son of Heaven in the dust wins no rain.'

sentences.zh: 又下州郡祈神致請。

translations.literal: Moreover you have ordered the provinces and commanderies to pray to the spirits and extend requests.
translations.idiomatic: Ordering the provinces to sacrifice to gods will not end the drought.

sentences.zh: 昔齊有大旱，景公欲祀河伯，晏子諫曰：『不可。

translations.literal: In antiquity Qi had a great drought; Duke Jing wished to sacrifice to the River Earl; Yanzi remonstrated, saying: 'It cannot be done.
translations.idiomatic: When Qi suffered drought, Duke Jing wanted to sacrifice to the river god; Yanzi said no.'

sentences.zh: 夫河伯以水為城國，魚□為民庶。

translations.literal: The River Earl takes water as walls and capital; fish and [lacuna] are his common people.
translations.idiomatic: 'The river god's realm is water; fish and turtles are his people.'

sentences.zh: 水盡魚枯，豈不欲雨？

translations.literal: When the water is exhausted and the fish are withered, how could he not desire rain?
translations.idiomatic: 'When the river runs dry, would he not want rain as much as we?'

sentences.zh: 自是不能致也。

translations.literal: From this he could not bring it about."
translations.idiomatic: 'Yet he cannot make it fall.'

sentences.zh: 』[八] 陛下所行，但務其華，不尋其實，猶緣木希魚，□行求前。

translations.literal: '[8] What Your Majesty practices only pursues its flourish and does not seek its substance; it is like climbing a tree to seek fish, or [lacuna] walking to seek front.
translations.idiomatic: [8] Your Majesty chases show, not substance—like climbing a tree for fish or walking backward to advance.'

sentences.zh: [九]誠宜推信革政，崇道變惑，出後宮不御之女，理天下冤枉之獄，除太官重膳之費。

translations.literal: [9] Truly it is fitting to extend trust, reform government, honor the Way and change delusion, release the women of the rear palace who are not visited, straighten the wrongly judged cases in the realm under Heaven, and remove the Grand Provisioner's heavy banquet expenses.
translations.idiomatic: [9] Extend good faith, reform the administration, elevate the Way, dismiss unused concubines, clear false convictions, and cut the lavish court kitchen.'

sentences.zh: 夫五品不訓，責在司徒，有非其位，宜急黜斥。

translations.literal: As for the five grades not being instructed, the blame lies with the Minister of Education; if there are those not equal to their posts, it is fitting urgently to dismiss and reject them.
translations.idiomatic: If the five relationships go untaught, fault the Minister of Education; remove men unfit for their posts at once.'

sentences.zh: 臣自藩外擢典納言，學薄智淺，不足以對。

translations.literal: Your subject, from outside the screen having been raised to hold the intake of words, is shallow in learning and shallow in wisdom, and is not sufficient to answer."
translations.idiomatic: Raised from a provincial post to this speaking role, your servant admits his learning is thin and his judgment slight—he cannot exhaust the topic in one reply.'

sentences.zh: 易傳曰：『陽感天，不旋日。

translations.literal: The tradition on the Changes says: 'Yang moves Heaven—not rotating a day.
translations.idiomatic: The Yi zhuan says, 'When yang stirs Heaven, the response comes within the day.'

sentences.zh: 』[一０]惟陛下留神裁察。

translations.literal: '[10] Only may Your Majesty pause and examine and decide."
translations.idiomatic: [10] May Your Majesty weigh these words.'"

sentences.zh: 」因召見舉及尚書令成翊世、僕射黃瓊，問以得失。

translations.literal: " Thereupon he summoned Ju to audience together with Director of the Masters of Writing Cheng Yishi and Vice Director Huang Qiong, and asked them about gains and losses.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor then called in Zhou Ju, Director Cheng Yishi, and Vice Director Huang Qiong to hear their verdict on what was wrong with the government.

sentences.zh: 舉等並對以為宜慎官人，去斥貪污，離遠佞邪，循文帝之儉，尊孝明之教，則時雨必應。

translations.literal: Ju and the others together replied, thinking it fitting to be careful in appointing men, remove and cast out greed and corruption, keep distant from flattery and evil, follow Emperor Wen's frugality, honor Emperor Xiaoming's instruction—then timely rain will surely respond.
translations.idiomatic: They agreed that the court must pick officials with care, purge the venal, shun flatterers, revive Emperor Wen's austerity and Emperor Ming's moral teaching—then the skies would answer with rain.

sentences.zh: 帝曰：「百官貪污佞邪者為誰乎？

translations.literal: The Emperor said: "Among the hundred offices, who are the greedy, corrupt, flattery, and evil?"
translations.idiomatic: The emperor asked, "Which of my ministers are corrupt toadies?"

sentences.zh: 」舉獨對曰：「臣從下州，超備機密，不足以別腢臣。

translations.literal: " Ju alone replied, saying: "Your subject came from a low province and leaped to fill a secret post; I am not sufficient to distinguish the multitude of ministers.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Ju answered alone: "I rose from a remote province to a confidential post—I cannot read every heart at court.

sentences.zh: [一一]然公卿大臣數有直言者，忠貞也；

translations.literal: [11] Yet among dukes and ministers those who repeatedly have straight words are loyal and upright;
translations.idiomatic: [11] Still, those who speak plainly are loyal;

sentences.zh: 阿諛苟容者，佞邪也。

translations.literal: those who flatter and comply for the sake of acceptance are flattery and evil.
translations.idiomatic: those who fawn for safety are the toadies."

sentences.zh: 司徒視事六年，未聞有忠言異謀，愚心在此。」

translations.literal: The Minister of Education has held office six years; I have not heard loyal words or unusual plans—your subject's foolish heart suspects this man."
translations.idiomatic: The Minister of Education has sat six years without a single searching memorial—in my poor judgment he is suspect."

sentences.zh: 其後以事免司徒劉崎，遷舉司隸校尉。

translations.literal: Afterward, because of an affair, he dismissed Minister of Education Liu Qi and promoted Ju to Metropolitan Superintendent.
translations.idiomatic: Soon afterward Liu Qi was stripped of the ministry and Zhou Ju became metropolitan superintendent.

sentences.zh: 注[一]天統、地統、人統謂之三統。

translations.literal: Note [1] The celestial system, terrestrial system, and human system are called the three systems.
translations.idiomatic: Note [1]: the three cosmic orders—Heaven, earth, and mankind.

sentences.zh: 事見白武通。

translations.literal: The affair is seen in the Baihu tong.
translations.idiomatic: See the Comprehensive Discussions in the White Tiger Hall.

sentences.zh: 注[二]尚書洪範曰：「建用皇極。

translations.literal: Note [2] The Documents, "Great Plan," says: "Establish and use the great mean.
translations.idiomatic: Note [2]: the Book of Documents, "Great Plan," says, "Take the great mean as your standard.

sentences.zh: 」孔安國注云：「皇，大也。

translations.literal: " Kong Anguo's commentary says: "Huang means great.
translations.idiomatic: " Kong Anguo glosses: "Huang means great.

sentences.zh: 極，中也。

translations.literal: Ji means center.
translations.idiomatic: Ji means the center.

sentences.zh: 言立大中之道而行之也。」

translations.literal: It means speaking of establishing the way of the great mean and practicing it."
translations.idiomatic: That is: set up the middle way and walk in it."

sentences.zh: 注[三]五品，五常之教也。

translations.literal: Note [3] The five grades are the teaching of the five constants.
translations.idiomatic: Note [3]: the "five grades" are the five moral relationships.

sentences.zh: 書曰：「五品不遜，汝作司徒，敬敷五教在寬。

translations.literal: The Documents says: "The five grades are not compliant—you shall be Minister of Education; respectfully spread the five teachings with lenience.
translations.idiomatic: The Documents reads, "When the five relationships fail, you, as Minister of Education, must spread the five teachings in gentleness.

sentences.zh: 」訓亦遜之義。

translations.literal: " Xun also has the meaning of compliant.
translations.idiomatic: " Here "xun" means compliant or yielding.

sentences.zh: 注[四]歿，終也。

translations.literal: Note [4] Mo means to end.
translations.idiomatic: Note [4]: "mo" means to die or end.

sentences.zh: 注[五]帝王紀曰：「武王入殷，命召公釋箕子之囚，表商容之閭，出傾宮之女於諸侯。」

translations.literal: Note [5] The Diwang ji says: "When King Wu entered Yin, he ordered the Duke of Shao to release Jizi from prison, marked the lane of Shang Rong, and released the women of the Tilted Palace to the feudal lords."
translations.idiomatic: Note [5]: the Di wang ji records King Wu freeing Jizi, honoring Shang Rong, and sending Shang's harem women back to the regional lords.

sentences.zh: 注[六]帝王紀曰：「湯伐桀，後大旱七年，洛川竭，使人持三足鼎祝於山川曰：

translations.literal: Note [6] The Diwang ji says: "When Tang attacked Jie, afterward there was great drought for seven years; the Luo river dried up; he sent men holding a three-legged cauldron to pray to mountains and rivers, saying:
translations.idiomatic: Note [6]: the Di wang ji says that after Tang overthrew Jie, seven years of drought emptied the Luo until Tang sent envoys with a tripod to pray:

sentences.zh: 『政不節邪？

translations.literal: 'Is government not restrained?
translations.idiomatic: "Is my government ill-disciplined?

sentences.zh: 使人疾邪？

translations.literal: Do men suffer?
translations.idiomatic: Are the people overworked?

sentences.zh: 苞苴行邪？

translations.literal: Do bribes run?
translations.idiomatic: Do bribes run unchecked?

sentences.zh: 讒夫昌邪？

translations.literal: Do slanderers flourish?
translations.idiomatic: Do slanderers thrive?

sentences.zh: 宮室榮邪？

translations.literal: Are palaces extravagant?
translations.idiomatic: Are palaces too splendid?

sentences.zh: 女謁行邪？

translations.literal: Do women's pleas operate?
translations.idiomatic: Do palace women peddle influence?

sentences.zh: 何不雨之極也！

translations.literal: Why is it not raining to the utmost!"
translations.idiomatic: Why then does Heaven withhold every drop!"

sentences.zh: 』」注[七]解見楊厚傳。

translations.literal: '" Note [7] The explanation is seen in the biography of Yang Hou.
translations.idiomatic: End quote." Note [7]: see Yang Hou's biography.

sentences.zh: 注[八]晏子春秋之文。

translations.literal: Note [8] It is text from the Yanzi chunqiu.
translations.idiomatic: Note [8]: from the Yanzi spring and autumn annals.

sentences.zh: 注[九]緣木求魚，見孟子之文。

translations.literal: Note [9] Climbing a tree to seek fish is seen in the text of Mencius.
translations.idiomatic: Note [9]: "climbing a tree for fish" comes from Mencius.

sentences.zh: 韓詩外傳曰：「夫明鏡所以照形，往古所以知今。

translations.literal: The Outer Commentary to the Han Poetry says: "As the bright mirror is for reflecting the form, antiquity is for knowing the present.
translations.idiomatic: The Han shi waizhuan says, "As a bright mirror shows the face, antiquity shows the present.

sentences.zh: 夫惡知往古之所以危亡，無異□行而求逮於前人也。」

translations.literal: How can one know why antiquity perished—no different from [lacuna] walking and seeking to catch up with those before?"
translations.idiomatic: How else explain past dynasties' fall—unless it is like walking backward yet hoping to outpace those ahead?"

sentences.zh: 注[一０]易稽覽圖之文也。

translations.literal: Note [10] It is text from the Changes, "Investigation of the Chart."
translations.idiomatic: Note [10]: from the Yi jilan tu.

sentences.zh: 解具郎顗傳也。

translations.literal: The explanation in full is in the biography of Lang Yi.
translations.idiomatic: Full discussion appears in Lang Yi's biography.

sentences.zh: 注[一一]別音彼列反。

translations.literal: Note [11] Bie is read bi lie fan.
translations.idiomatic: Note [11]: "bie" is read bi lie fan.

sentences.zh: 注[二]杜預注左傳曰：「短折曰夭，未名曰昏。」

translations.literal: Note [2] Du Yu's commentary on the Zuo Tradition says: "Short break is called yao; not yet named is called hun."
translations.idiomatic: Note [2]: Du Yu defines yao as early death and hun as dying unnamed.

sentences.zh: 注[二]纂文曰：「□露，今之輓歌也。

translations.literal: Note [2] The Zuanwen says: "[Xie]lu is what is today the dirge song.
translations.idiomatic: Note [2]: the Zuanwen calls the funeral hymn xielu—the "dew on the leek" dirge.

sentences.zh: 」崔豹古今注□露歌曰：「□上露何易唏！

translations.literal: " Cui Bao's Gujin zhu on the Xielu song says: "[Xie] on top, dew—how easily it dries!
translations.idiomatic: Cui Bao's Gujin zhu quotes the song: "Dew on the leek dries so fast!

sentences.zh: 露晞明朝還復落，人死一去何時歸？」

translations.literal: When the dew dries, tomorrow morning it will again fall; when a man dies once gone, when will he return?"
translations.idiomatic: The sun burns it off, yet dew returns at dawn—but the dead do not come back."

sentences.zh: 注[三]左傳曰，叔孫昭子與宋公語，相泣。

translations.literal: Note [3] The Zuo Tradition says that Shusun Zhaozi spoke with the Duke of Song, and they wept together.
translations.idiomatic: Note [3]: the Zuo zhuan tells how Shusun Zhaozi and the Duke of Song wept together.

sentences.zh: 樂祁退而告人曰：「君與叔孫其皆死乎？

translations.literal: Yue Qi withdrew and told others, saying: "The ruler and Shusun will both die, will they not?
translations.idiomatic: Yue Qi left and said, "Lord and minister will both die.

sentences.zh: 吾聞之，哀樂而樂哀，皆喪心也。

translations.literal: I have heard that to grieve and then rejoice in joy, and to rejoice and then grieve in grief—both are loss of mind.
translations.idiomatic: I have heard: grief that turns to joy, or joy that turns to grief, means the heart has left its seat.

sentences.zh: 心之精爽，是謂魂魄。

translations.literal: The essence and brightness of the mind—this is called hun and po.
translations.idiomatic: The vital brightness of the mind is the hun and po souls.

sentences.zh: 魂魄去之，何以能久也！」

translations.literal: When hun and po depart from it, how can one long endure!"
translations.idiomatic: When those souls flee, how long can the body last!"

sentences.zh: 時連有□異，帝思商言，召舉於顯親殿，問以變眚。

translations.literal: At the time there were successive [lacuna] anomalies; the Emperor thought on Shang's words and summoned Ju in the Hall of Manifest Kindred, asking about changes and calamities.
translations.idiomatic: Portents piled up until the emperor recalled Zhao Shang's earlier advice and summoned Zhou Ju to the Hall of Manifest Kin to ask what the omens meant.

sentences.zh: 舉對曰：「陛下初立，遵修舊典，興化致政，遠近肅然。

translations.literal: Ju replied, saying: "When Your Majesty first ascended, you followed and repaired the old canons, raised transformation and extended government; far and near were solemnly ordered.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Ju answered: "At your accession you restored the old statutes and spread reform—order prevailed from the capital to the marches.

sentences.zh: 頃年以來，稍違於前，朝多寵幸，祿不序德。

translations.literal: In recent years you have gradually departed from what was before; the court has much favor and fortune, and salaries do not follow virtue.
translations.idiomatic: Lately policy has drifted: favorites multiply, and rank no longer tracks merit.

sentences.zh: 觀天察人，准今方古，誠可危懼。

translations.literal: Observing Heaven and examining men, matching today against antiquity, it is truly something to fear.
translations.idiomatic: Read Heaven, read men, compare today with yesterday—the picture is frightening.

sentences.zh: 書曰：『僭恆暘若。

translations.literal: The Documents says: 'If presumption is constant, then drought follows.
translations.idiomatic: The Documents warns: "Persistent arrogance brings drought.

sentences.zh: 』[一]夫僭差無度，則言不從而下不正；

translations.literal: '[1] When presumption and error are without measure, then words are not obeyed and what is below is not straight;
translations.idiomatic: [1] When excess knows no limit, orders fail and the base goes crooked;

sentences.zh: 陽無以制，則上擾下竭。

translations.literal: when yang has nothing to control it, then above is disturbed and below is exhausted.
translations.idiomatic: when unchecked yang rages, high and low both suffer."

sentences.zh: 宜密嚴□州郡，察強宗大奸，以時禽討。」

translations.literal: It is fitting secretly to be strict with the provinces and commanderies, investigate powerful lineages and great villains, and in due season capture and punish them."
translations.idiomatic: Secretly tighten control on the provinces, root out great clans and arch-criminals, and strike when the moment is ripe."

sentences.zh: 其後江淮猾賊周生、徐鳳等處處並起，如舉所陳。

translations.literal: Afterward the crafty bandits of the Yangzi and Huai, Zhou Sheng, Xu Feng, and others, everywhere rose together, as Ju had stated.
translations.idiomatic: Soon the Huai bandits Zhou Sheng and Xu Feng rose everywhere, just as Zhou Ju had warned.

sentences.zh: 注[一]尚書洪範之文也。

translations.literal: Note [1] It is text from the Documents, "Great Plan."
translations.idiomatic: Note [1]: from the "Great Plan" in the Book of Documents.

sentences.zh: 孔安國注曰：「君行僭差，則常暘順之也。」

translations.literal: Kong Anguo's commentary says: "If the ruler's conduct is presumptuous and erring, then constant drought follows it."
translations.idiomatic: Kong Anguo adds: "When the ruler strays from the mean, long drought follows."

sentences.zh: 時詔遣八使巡行風俗，皆選素有威名者，乃拜舉為侍中，舉侍中杜喬、守光祿大夫周栩、前青州刺史馮羨、尚書欒巴、侍御史張綱、兗州刺史郭遵、太尉長史劉班並守光祿大夫，分行天下。

translations.literal: At the time an edict sent eight envoys to tour and observe customs; all were chosen from those who in the past had awe-inspiring reputation; thereupon Ju was appointed palace attendant; palace attendant Du Qiao, acting Grand Master of Splendid Carriages Zhou Xu, former Governor of Qing Feng Xian, Master of Writing Luan Ba, attendant censor Zhang Gang, Governor of Yan Guo Zun, chief clerk of the Grand Commandant Liu Ban—together acting as Grand Masters of Splendid Carriages—divided and went through the realm under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: An edict then dispatched eight inspectors, each a man of proven sternness: Zhou Ju and Du Qiao as palace attendants, Zhou Xu and others as acting grand masters of splendid carriages—they fanned out across the empire.

sentences.zh: 其刺史、二千石有臧罪顯明者，驛馬上之；

translations.literal: As for governors and two-thousand-dan who had manifest crimes of corruption, relay horses to memorialize upward;
translations.idiomatic: Any governor or two-thousand-dan with clear evidence of graft was to be reported by post horse;

sentences.zh: 墨綬以下，便輒收舉。

translations.literal: below the black ribbon, then on the spot arrest and impeach.
translations.idiomatic: magistrates in the black sash could be seized on the spot.

sentences.zh: 其有清忠惠利，為百姓所安，宜表異者，皆以狀上。

translations.literal: As for those who were pure, loyal, kindly, and beneficial, whom the hundred surnames found peaceful—it was fitting to mark them as unusual—all should be submitted upward in memorial form.
translations.idiomatic: Pure, useful magistrates beloved of the people were to be singled out in memorials.

sentences.zh: 於是八使同時俱拜，天下號曰「八俊」。

translations.literal: Thereupon the eight envoys at the same time all received appointment; the realm under Heaven called them the "Eight Paragons."
translations.idiomatic: The eight men took office together and the empire dubbed them the "Eight Paragons."

sentences.zh: 舉於是劾奏貪猾，表薦公清，朝廷稱之。

translations.literal: Ju thereupon impeached the greedy and slippery, and in memorials recommended the public-minded and pure; the court praised him.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Ju impeached the corrupt and recommended the clean; the court applauded him.

sentences.zh: 遷河內太守，征為大鴻臚。

translations.literal: He was promoted Grand Administrator of Henei and summoned to be Grand Herald.
translations.idiomatic: He became governor of Henei, then was recalled as grand herald.

sentences.zh: 及梁太后臨朝，詔以殤帝幼崩，廟次宜在順帝下。

translations.literal: When Empress Dowager Liang faced the court, an edict said that because the Emperor Shang was young and died, his place in the temple sequence ought to be below Emperor Shun.
translations.idiomatic: When Empress Dowager Liang ruled, an edict argued that the infant Emperor Shang, having died young, should rank below Emperor Shun in the ancestral temple.

sentences.zh: 太常馬訪奏宜如詔書，諫議大夫呂勃以為應依昭穆之序，先殤帝，後順帝。

translations.literal: Minister of Ceremonies Ma Fang memorialized that it ought to be as the edict; remonstrance counsellor Lü Bo thought one should follow the order of zhao and mu, first Emperor Shang, afterward Emperor Shun.
translations.idiomatic: Minister Ma Fang backed the edict; Counselor Lü Bo insisted on proper zhao-mu order—Shang before Shun.

sentences.zh: 詔下公卿。

translations.literal: An edict sent it down to the dukes and ministers.
translations.idiomatic: The matter went to the high ministers for debate.

sentences.zh: 舉議曰：「春秋魯閔公無子，庶兄僖公代立，其子文公遂躋僖於閔上。

translations.literal: Ju opined, saying: "In the Spring and Autumn, Duke Min of Lu had no son; his younger brother by a concubine, Duke Xi, replaced him; his son Duke Wen thereupon placed Xi above Min.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Ju argued: "The Spring and Autumn records that Duke Min of Lu died without an heir and Duke Xi succeeded; Duke Wen later ranked his father Xi above Min.

sentences.zh: 孔子譏之，書曰：『有事於太廟，躋僖公。

translations.literal: Confucius criticized it; the document says: 'There was a sacrifice in the Grand Temple—Xi was placed above Min.
translations.idiomatic: Confucius condemned this with the line, "They sacrificed in the Grand Temple and ranked Xi above Min.

sentences.zh: 』傳曰：『逆祀也。

translations.literal: ' The tradition says: 'It was inverted sacrifice.
translations.idiomatic: The gloss calls it inverted sacrifice.

sentences.zh: 』[一]及定公正其序，經曰『從祀先公』，為萬世法也。

translations.literal: '[1] When Duke Ding corrected their order, the classic says "follow sacrifice to the former dukes"—it is the model for ten thousand generations.
translations.idiomatic: [1] When Duke Ding restored the proper order, the classic read "orderly sacrifice to the former dukes"—that is the pattern for all ages.

sentences.zh: [二]今殤帝在先，於秩為父，順帝在後，於親為子，先後之義不可改，昭穆之序不可亂。

translations.literal: [2] Now Emperor Shang is before, in rank he is father; Emperor Shun is after, in kinship he is son—the meaning of before and after cannot be altered, the order of zhao and mu cannot be confused.
translations.idiomatic: [2] Emperor Shang precedes Shun as a father in rank; Shun follows as son—the sequence cannot be reversed without breaking zhao-mu.

sentences.zh: 呂勃議是也。

translations.literal: Lü Bo's opinion is correct.
translations.idiomatic: Lü Bo is right."

sentences.zh: 」太后下詔從之。

translations.literal: " The Empress Dowager issued an edict following it.
translations.idiomatic: The empress dowager issued an edict agreeing.

sentences.zh: 遷光祿勳，會遭母憂去職，後拜光祿大夫。

translations.literal: He was promoted Director of the Masters of Splendid Carriages; he met with mourning for his mother and left office; afterward he was appointed Grand Master of Splendid Carriages.
translations.idiomatic: He rose to director of splendid carriages, resigned for his mother's funeral, then returned as grand master of splendid carriages.

sentences.zh: 注[一]事見左氏傳。

translations.literal: Note [1] The affair is seen in the Zuo Tradition.
translations.idiomatic: Note [1]: see the Zuo zhuan.

sentences.zh: 注[二]左氏傳：「從祀先公。

translations.literal: Note [2] The Zuo Tradition: "Follow sacrifice to the former dukes.
translations.idiomatic: Note [2]: the Zuo reads "orderly sacrifice to the former dukes.

sentences.zh: 」杜預云：「從，順也。

translations.literal: " Du Yu says: "Cong means to follow.
translations.idiomatic: " Du Yu: "cong" means to set in proper order.

sentences.zh: 先公，閔公、僖公也。

translations.literal: The former dukes are Duke Min and Duke Xi.
translations.idiomatic: The 'former dukes' are Dukes Min and Xi of Lu.

sentences.zh: 將正二公之位，親盡，故通言先公也。」

translations.literal: He was about to correct the positions of the two dukes; kin was exhausted, therefore he spoke in general of the former dukes."
translations.idiomatic: Duke Ding was reordering their tablets; because the closer generations had ended, the text speaks broadly of the former dukes."

sentences.zh: 建和三年卒。

translations.literal: In the third year of Jianhe he died.
translations.idiomatic: He died in Jianhe 3.

sentences.zh: 朝廷以舉清公亮直，方欲以為宰相，深痛惜之。

translations.literal: The court, because Ju was pure, public, bright, and straight, was about to make him chancellor, and deeply grieved and pitied him.
translations.idiomatic: The court had meant to make him chancellor—his death was a bitter loss.

sentences.zh: 乃詔告光祿勳、汝南太守曰：「昔在前世，求賢如渴，封墓軾閭，以光賢哲。

translations.literal: Thereupon an edict announced to the Director of the Masters of Splendid Carriages and the Grand Administrator of Runan, saying: "In former ages they sought the worthy as if thirsty, sealed tombs and brushed lintels, thereby to brighten the worthy and wise.
translations.idiomatic: An edict to the director of splendid carriages and the Runan governor read: "Ancient kings hunted talent as if parched; they honored the dead by tending tombs and brushing doorposts.

sentences.zh: [一]故公叔見誄，翁歸蒙述，所以昭忠厲俗，作范後昆。

translations.literal: [1] Therefore when Gongshu received dirge, Wengui received narrative—this is how to display loyalty and encourage custom, to make a model for later descendants.
translations.idiomatic: [1] Hence dirges for Gongshu and praise for Wengui—to publish loyalty and teach the age.

sentences.zh: [二]故光祿大夫周舉，性侔夷、魚，[三] 忠踰隨、管，[四]前授牧守，及還納言，出入京輦，有欽哉之績，[五]在禁闈有密靜之風。

translations.literal: [2] Therefore Grand Master of Splendid Carriages Zhou Ju, his nature equal to Yi and Yu, [3] his loyalty exceeding Sui and Guan, [4] formerly appointed shepherd and guard, and afterward returning to hold the intake of words, coming in and out of the capital and imperial carriage—he had the merit of "reverently yes"; [5] in the forbidden gates he had the wind of secret quiet.
translations.idiomatic: [2] Grand Master Zhou Ju matched Boyi and Shuqi in purity, [3] surpassed Sui Hui and Guan Zhong in loyalty, [4] served as regional governor and at court rode the imperial equipage with "reverent care," [5] and moved through the palace in calm discretion.

sentences.zh: 予錄乃勳，用登九列。

translations.literal: We record your merit, thereby to ascend the nine ranks.
translations.idiomatic: We record his merit and raise his rank among the nine honors.

sentences.zh: 方欲式序百官，亮協三事，不永夙終，用乖遠圖。

translations.literal: We were about to array the hundred offices and brighten and harmonize the three affairs—he did not live out the morning, and thereby the distant plan was thwarted.
translations.idiomatic: We meant to array the bureaucracy and harmonize the three councils—he died too soon, and long-range plans collapsed.

sentences.zh: 朝廷愍悼，良為愴然。

translations.literal: The court pitied and mourned; truly it was mournful.
translations.idiomatic: The court mourns him with genuine grief.

sentences.zh: 詩不雲乎：『肇敏戎功，用錫爾祉。

translations.literal: Does not the Poetry say: 'Begin diligently the martial merit, thereby to extend your blessing.
translations.idiomatic: The Poetry says, "Work hard at the martial charge and Heaven will extend your blessing.

sentences.zh: 』[六]其令將大夫以下到喪發日復會吊。

translations.literal: '[6] Order that from generals and grandees downward, until the day the funeral sets out, they again assemble to condole.
translations.idiomatic: [6] Let every officer down to the generals gather again on the day the catafalque sets out.

sentences.zh: 加賜錢十萬，以旌委蛇素絲之節焉。

translations.literal: Add a gift of one hundred thousand cash, in order to signal the integrity of the winding kudzu and plain silk.
translations.idiomatic: Add a hundred thousand cash to honor his winding-kudzu, plain-silk integrity.

sentences.zh: 」[七]子勰。

translations.literal: "[7] His son was Xie.
translations.idiomatic: [7] His son was Zhou Xie.

sentences.zh: [八] 注[一]尚書曰，武王入殷，封比干墓，軾商容閭。

translations.literal: [8] Note [1] The Documents says that when King Wu entered Yin, he sealed Bi Gan's tomb and brushed Shang Rong's lintel.
translations.idiomatic: [8] Note [1]: the Documents says King Wu tended Bi Gan's grave and honored Shang Rong's door.

sentences.zh: 注[二]公叔文子，衛大夫也。

translations.literal: Note [2] Gongshu Wenzi was a grandee of Wei.
translations.idiomatic: Note [2]: Gongshu Wenzi was a Wei minister.

sentences.zh: 文子卒，其子戌請謚於君。

translations.literal: When Wenzi died, his son Xu requested a posthumous title from the ruler.
translations.idiomatic: When he died, his son Xu asked the duke for a posthumous name.

sentences.zh: 君曰：「昔者衛國凶饑，夫子為粥與國之餓者，不亦惠乎？

translations.literal: The ruler said: "In antiquity when Wei had famine and dearth, the master made gruel and gave it to the starving of the state—is this not kindly?
translations.idiomatic: The duke said, "When Wei starved, he fed the hungry—is that not kindness?

sentences.zh: 衛國有難，夫子以其死衛寡人，不亦貞乎？

translations.literal: When Wei had disaster, the master with his death guarded me, the lonely one—is this not loyal?
translations.idiomatic: When Wei was in peril, he died for his lord—is that not loyalty?

sentences.zh: 夫子聽衛國之政，修其班制，不亦文乎？

translations.literal: The master heard Wei's government, cultivated its ranks and regulations—is this not cultured?
translations.idiomatic: He put Wei's government in order—is that not refinement?

sentences.zh: 謂夫子『貞惠文子』。

translations.literal: We call the master 'Loyal, Kindly, Cultured Master.'
translations.idiomatic: Call him the Loyal, Kind, Refined Master.

sentences.zh: 」事見禮記。

translations.literal: " The affair is seen in the Record of Rites.
translations.idiomatic: The story is in the Book of Rites.

sentences.zh: 尹翁歸為右扶風，*[卒]*，宣帝下詔□揚，賜金百斤。

translations.literal: Yin Wengui was Governor of You Fufeng; *[when he died]*, Emperor Xuan issued an edict [lacuna] praising him and bestowed one hundred jin of gold.
translations.idiomatic: Yin Wengui, governor of You Fufeng, died in office; Emperor Xuan issued an edict of praise and sent a hundred jin of gold.

sentences.zh: 班固曰：「翁歸承風，帝揚厥聲。

translations.literal: Ban Gu says: "Wengui received the wind; the Emperor raised his fame."
translations.idiomatic: Ban Gu wrote, "Wengui caught the imperial breeze, and the throne magnified his name."

sentences.zh: 」故曰蒙述也。

translations.literal: " Therefore it is said he received narrative.
translations.idiomatic: Hence the phrase that the throne "spread his fame" in a written tribute.

sentences.zh: 注[三]伯夷、史魚也。

translations.literal: Note [3] Boyi and Shi Yu.
translations.idiomatic: Note [3]: Boyi and Shi Yu.

sentences.zh: 注[四]隨會、管仲。

translations.literal: Note [4] Sui Hui and Guan Zhong.
translations.idiomatic: Note [4]: Sui Hui and Guan Zhong.

sentences.zh: 注[五]史記堯典曰：「咨十有二牧，欽哉！」

translations.literal: Note [5] The Records of the Historian, "Canon of Yao," says: "Consult the twelve shepherds—reverently yes!"
translations.idiomatic: Note [5]: the Shiji "Yao dian" has Yao telling the twelve shepherds, "Be reverent in your charge!"

sentences.zh: 注[六]詩大雅也。

translations.literal: Note [6] It is an ode in the Major Odes of the Poetry.
translations.idiomatic: Note [6]: from the "Major Odes" of the Classic of Poetry.

sentences.zh: 肇，謀也。

translations.literal: Zhao means to plan.
translations.idiomatic: "Zhao" means to plan.

sentences.zh: 敏，疾也。

translations.literal: Min means swift.
translations.idiomatic: "Min" means swift or diligent.

sentences.zh: 戎，汝也。

translations.literal: Rong means you.
translations.idiomatic: "Rong" is an old form of "you."

sentences.zh: 錫，賜也。

translations.literal: Xi means to bestow.
translations.idiomatic: "Xi" means to grant.

sentences.zh: 祉，福也。

translations.literal: Zhi means blessing.
translations.idiomatic: "Zhi" means blessing.

sentences.zh: 注[七]*

translations.literal: Commentary note seven (text damaged).
translations.idiomatic: Seventh commentary gloss; the following line is supplied from the Odes.

translations.literal: (Poetry)
translations.idiomatic: (from the Poetry)

sentences.zh: *國風羔羊詩：「羔羊之皮，素絲五紽。

translations.literal: *Airs of the States, "Lamb": "The lamb's skin, plain silk in five tassels.
translations.idiomatic: The "Lamb" ode in the Guofeng says: "Lamb's fleece, five white silk cords.

sentences.zh: 退食自公，逶蛇逶蛇。」

translations.literal: Withdrawing from the meal from the duke, winding and winding."
translations.idiomatic: They leave the lord's feast, sash swaying, easy and unhurried."

sentences.zh: 注[八]音□。

translations.literal: Note [8] The sound is [lacuna].
translations.idiomatic: Note [8]: pronunciation gloss (character damaged).

sentences.zh: 勰字巨勝，少尚玄虛，以父任為郎，自免歸家。

translations.literal: Xie's courtesy name was Jusheng; in youth he esteemed dark emptiness; because of his father's position he became a Gentleman, but dismissed himself and returned home.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Xie, courtesy Jusheng, favored Daoist quietism; he inherited a nominal court post as Gentleman, then resigned and went home.

sentences.zh: 父故吏河南召夔為郡將，卑身降禮，致敬於勰。

translations.literal: His father's former clerk Shao Kui of Henan was commandant of the commandery; lowering his person and humbling ritual, he paid respect to Xie.
translations.idiomatic: Shao Kui of Henan, once his father's subordinate and now commandant, humbled himself to honor Zhou Xie.

sentences.zh: 勰恥交報之，因杜門自絕。

translations.literal: Xie was ashamed to associate and repay him, and thereupon shut his gates and cut himself off.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Xie thought it improper to trade courtesies and barred his gate.

sentences.zh: 後太守舉孝廉，復以疾去。

translations.literal: Afterward the grand administrator recommended him as filial and incorrupt; again because of illness he departed.
translations.idiomatic: Later the governor nominated him as filial and incorrupt; he pleaded illness and declined.

sentences.zh: 時梁冀貴盛，被其征命者，莫敢不應，唯勰前後三辟，竟不能屈。

translations.literal: At the time Liang Ji was noble and flourishing; those who received his summons—none dared not respond; only Xie, front and back three summonses, in the end could not bend him.
translations.idiomatic: When Liang Ji was all-powerful, no one dared ignore his summons—yet Zhou Xie refused three separate calls.

sentences.zh: 後舉賢良方正，不應。

translations.literal: Afterward he was recommended as worthy, good, and upright; he did not respond.
translations.idiomatic: He was later nominated as worthy and upright but did not answer.

sentences.zh: 又公車征，玄纁備禮，固辭廢疾。

translations.literal: Again the public carriage summoned him; dark and light silks were complete in ritual; he firmly declined on grounds of chronic illness.
translations.idiomatic: Another imperial carriage summons came with full black-and-yellow gifts; he pleaded chronic infirmity and refused.

sentences.zh: 常隱處竄身，慕老聃清靜，杜絕人事，巷生荊棘，十有餘歲。

translations.literal: He constantly hid himself in seclusion, admired Laozi's purity and quiet, cut off human affairs; brambles grew in the lanes for more than ten years.
translations.idiomatic: He lived in hiding, modeled Laozi's stillness, broke off all social ties, until brambles choked his alley for a decade.

sentences.zh: 至延熹二年，乃開門延賓，游談宴樂，及秋而梁冀誅，年終而勰卒，時年五十。

translations.literal: Down to the second year of Yanxi, he then opened his gate and extended welcome to guests, roaming in talk, feasting and pleasure; by autumn Liang Ji was executed; at year's end Xie died, aged fifty sui.
translations.idiomatic: In Yanxi 2 he reopened his door to guests and conversation; that autumn Liang Ji fell; by year's end Zhou Xie was dead at fifty.

sentences.zh: 蔡邕以為知命。

translations.literal: Cai Yong thought he knew Heaven's mandate.
translations.idiomatic: Cai Yong said he had read his fate aright.

sentences.zh: 自勰曾祖父揚至勰孫恂，六世一身，皆知名雲。

translations.literal: From Xie's great-grandfather Yang down to Xie's grandson Xun, six generations in one line—all were famous in the clouds.
translations.idiomatic: From his great-grandfather Zhou Yang through his grandson Zhou Xun, six generations in direct line won renown.

sentences.zh: 黃瓊字世英，江夏安陸人，魏郡太守香之子也。

translations.literal: Huang Qiong, courtesy name Shiying, was a man of Anlu in Jiangxia, son of Grand Administrator Xiang of Wei commandery.
translations.idiomatic: Huang Qiong, courtesy Shiying, came from Anlu in Jiangxia; his father Xiang had governed Wei commandery.

sentences.zh: 香在文苑傳。

translations.literal: Xiang is in the biography of the Literary Garden.
translations.idiomatic: Huang Xiang has a separate entry among the literati.

sentences.zh: 瓊初以父任為太子捨人，辭病不就。

translations.literal: Qiong at first because of his father's position became attendant to the heir apparent; he declined illness and did not take office.
translations.idiomatic: He first received a nominal post as heir apparent's attendant but pleaded illness and never served.

sentences.zh: 遭父憂，服闋，五府俱辟，連年不應。

translations.literal: He met mourning for his father; when the mourning period ended, the five offices together summoned him; for successive years he did not respond.
translations.idiomatic: After his father's death and the end of mourning, all five chief bureaus summoned him year after year; he ignored every call.

sentences.zh: 永建中，公卿多薦瓊者，於是與會稽賀純、廣漢楊厚俱公車征。

translations.literal: In the Yongjian period, dukes and ministers many recommended Qiong; thereupon together with He Chun of Kuaiji and Yang Hou of Guanghan he was summoned by public carriage.
translations.idiomatic: During Yongjian high ministers repeatedly recommended him, and he was finally summoned by carriage along with He Chun of Kuaiji and Yang Hou of Guanghan.

sentences.zh: 瓊至綸氏，稱疾不進。

translations.literal: When Qiong reached Lunshi, he claimed illness and did not advance.
translations.idiomatic: At Lunshi he halted, pleading sickness.

sentences.zh: [一]有司劾不敬，詔下縣以禮慰遣，遂不得已。

translations.literal: [1] The responsible offices impeached him for lack of respect; an edict ordered the county to comfort and send him off with ritual; thereupon he had no choice.
translations.idiomatic: [1] Officials impeached him for disrespect; the emperor ordered the county to escort him courteously—he had to go.

sentences.zh: 先是徵聘處士多不稱望，李固素慕於瓊，乃以書逆遺之曰：「聞已度伊、洛，近在萬歲亭，豈即事有漸，將順王命乎？

translations.literal: Before this, summoned recluses mostly did not match expectations; Li Gu had long admired Qiong, and therefore sent him a letter beforehand, saying: "I hear you have already crossed the Yi and Luo, and are near the Wansui Pavilion—is it that matters have gradually progressed, and you will comply with the king's command?
translations.idiomatic: Earlier summonses had disappointed the court; Li Gu, who admired Huang Qiong, wrote ahead: "I hear you have crossed the Yi and Luo and neared Wansui Post—does this mean you are coming at last to obey the summons?

sentences.zh: [二]蓋君子謂伯夷隘，柳下惠不恭，故傳曰『不夷不惠，可否之閒』。

translations.literal: [2] The gentleman calls Boyi narrow and Liuxia Hui not respectful; therefore the tradition says "Neither Boyi nor Hui—between yes and no."
translations.idiomatic: [2] The gentleman finds Boyi too rigid and Liuxia Hui too easy; the adage runs, "Neither as harsh as Boyi nor as lax as Hui—stand in the workable middle."

sentences.zh: [三]蓋聖賢居身之所珍也。

translations.literal: [3] This is what the sages and worthies treasured in establishing themselves.
translations.idiomatic: [3] That middle ground is what the sages prized in their conduct.

sentences.zh: 誠遂欲枕山棲谷，擬跡巢、由，斯則可矣；

translations.literal: If you truly wish to pillow on mountains and roost in valleys, and trace the footprints of Chao and Xu—then it can be done;
translations.idiomatic: If you mean to sleep on cliffs and rival the recluses Chao and Xu, do so;

sentences.zh: 若當輔政濟民，今其時也。

translations.literal: if you would assist government and succor the people, now is the time.
translations.idiomatic: if you would govern and save the people, the moment is now.

sentences.zh: 自生民以來，善政少而亂俗多，必待堯舜之君，此為志士終無時矣。

translations.literal: From the birth of the people, good government has been rare and corrupt customs many; if one must wait for a Yao or Shun ruler, this means a man of purpose will never have his hour.
translations.idiomatic: Since history began, good rule has been scarce; wait only for another Yao or Shun and a true man never acts.

sentences.zh: 常聞語曰：『嶢嶢者易缺，皦皦者易污。

translations.literal: I have often heard the saying: "The towering are easily chipped; the dazzling are easily stained.
translations.idiomatic: A proverb says, "The peak that juts gets broken; the gleam that blinds draws mud."

sentences.zh: 』陽春之曲，和者必寡，盛名之下，其實難副。

translations.literal: "The song of Yangchun—those who harmonize must be few; beneath a great name, the reality is hard to match.
translations.idiomatic: "Yangchun" has few who can sing along; a towering reputation rarely matches the fact.

sentences.zh: [四]近魯陽樊君被征初至，朝廷設□席，猶待神明。

translations.literal: [4] Recently when Fan the lord of Luyang was first summoned and arrived, the court spread [lacuna] mats, as if awaiting a spirit.
translations.idiomatic: [4] When Fan Ying of Luyang first reached court, the throne spread a mat as for a god.

sentences.zh: [五]雖無大異，而言行所守無缺。

translations.literal: [5] Although there was no great difference, in word and deed what he guarded was without flaw.
translations.idiomatic: [5] He was no miracle worker, yet his conduct was blameless.

sentences.zh: 而毀謗布流，應時折減者，豈非觀聽望深，聲名太盛乎？

translations.literal: Yet slander spread like flowing water, and at the time his reputation was reduced—is it not that what was seen and heard hoped too deeply, and his fame and sound were too great?
translations.idiomatic: Still slander cut him down—because the court expected wonders and his fame had overshot reality?

sentences.zh: 自頃徵聘之士，胡元安、薛孟嘗、朱仲昭、顧季鴻等，其功業皆無所採，是故俗論皆言處士純盜虛聲。

translations.literal: From recent summoned scholars—Hu Yuanan, Xue Mengchang, Zhu Zhongzhao, Gu Jihong, and others—their achievements and enterprises had nothing to gather; therefore vulgar opinion all says recluses purely steal empty reputation.
translations.idiomatic: Recent hermits such as Hu Yuanan, Xue Mengchang, Zhu Zhongzhao, and Gu Jihong left no deeds—so gossip says every recluse is a fraud.

sentences.zh: 願先生弘此遠謨，令觿人歎服，一雪此言耳。

translations.literal: I hope the master will expand this far plan, causing the multitude to sigh in submission, and once for all wash away this saying.
translations.idiomatic: I hope you will vindicate the high path, silence the cynics, and clear that slur once for all."

sentences.zh: 」瓊至，即拜議郎，稍遷尚書僕射。

translations.literal: " When Qiong arrived, he was immediately appointed Gentleman Consultant and was gradually promoted to Vice Director of the Masters of Writing.
translations.idiomatic: Huang Qiong reached the capital, was made a Gentleman Consultant, and rose to vice director of the Masters of Writing.

sentences.zh: 注[一]綸氏即夏之綸國，少康之邑也。

translations.literal: Note [1] Lunshi was the Lun state of Xia, the town of Shaokang.
translations.idiomatic: Note [1]: Lunshi was ancient Lun of the Xia, Shaokang's town.

sentences.zh: 竹書紀年云：「楚及秦伐鄭綸氏。

translations.literal: The Bamboo Annals says: "Chu together with Qin attacked Zheng at Lunshi.
translations.idiomatic: The Zhushu jinian records Chu and Qin attacking Zheng at Lunshi.

sentences.zh: 」今洛州故嵩陽縣城是也。

translations.literal: " It is the old city of Songyang county in modern Luo prefecture.
translations.idiomatic: The site is the old Songyang county seat in modern Luoyang prefecture.

sentences.zh: 注[二]萬歲亭在今洛州故嵩陽縣西北。

translations.literal: Note [2] Wansui Pavilion is northwest of the old Songyang county in modern Luo prefecture.
translations.idiomatic: Note [2]: Wansui Post stood northwest of old Songyang.

sentences.zh: 武帝元封元年，幸緱氏，登太室，聞山上呼萬歲聲者三，因以名焉。

translations.literal: In the first year of Yuanfeng of Emperor Wu, he visited Gou'shi, ascended the Grand Chamber, and heard on the mountain the sound of "Wansui" three times; therefore he named it thus.
translations.idiomatic: In Han Wudi's Yuanfeng 1 he visited Gou'shi, climbed Mount Song, heard "long life!" cried thrice from the hills, and named the spot Wansui.

sentences.zh: 注[三]論語孔子曰，伯夷、叔齊不降其志，不辱其身。

translations.literal: Note [3] The Analects: Confucius says that Boyi and Shuqi did not lower their aims and did not disgrace their persons.
translations.idiomatic: Note [3]: the Analects praises Boyi and Shuqi for never debasing their purpose or person.

sentences.zh: 謂柳下惠、少連降志辱身。

translations.literal: He calls Liuxia Hui and Shaolian as lowering their aims and disgracing their persons.
translations.idiomatic: He classes Liuxia Hui and Shaolian as men who bent their aims and shamed themselves.

sentences.zh: 我則異於是，無可無不可。

translations.literal: I am different from this—there is nothing I must do and nothing I must not do.
translations.idiomatic: Confucius adds, "I am not like them—I am bound neither to yes nor to no."

sentences.zh: 鄭玄注云：不為夷、齊之清，不為惠、連之屈，故曰異於是也。

translations.literal: Zheng Xuan's commentary says: He did not make Boyi and Shuqi's purity, did not make Hui and Lian's bending; therefore he says he was different from this.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xuan glosses: Confucius would be neither as austere as Boyi nor as pliant as Hui—hence "different."

sentences.zh: 注[四]宋玉對楚襄王問曰：「客有歌於郢中者，為下裡巴人，國中屬而和者數千人；

translations.literal: Note [4] Song Yu, answering King Xiang of Chu's question, said: "A guest sang in Ying—if it was 'Lower Village, Ba People,' the state joined in by the thousands;
translations.idiomatic: Note [4]: Song Yu told King Xiang that a singer in Ying drew thousands for a crude tune.

sentences.zh: 為陽春白雪，屬而和者不過數百人。

translations.literal: if it was 'Yangchun, White Snow,' those who joined were not more than several hundred.
translations.idiomatic: When he sang "Yangchun" and "White Snow," only a few hundred could follow.

sentences.zh: 是其曲□高，其和□寡。」

translations.literal: It is that the song was high and the harmony was few."
translations.idiomatic: The loftier the song, the thinner the chorus."

sentences.zh: 注[五]樊君，樊英也。

translations.literal: Note [5] Lord Fan is Fan Ying.
translations.idiomatic: Note [5]: "Lord Fan" is Fan Ying.

sentences.zh: 事具英傳。

translations.literal: The affair in full is in Ying's biography.
translations.idiomatic: His story is told in Fan Ying's biography.

sentences.zh: 初，瓊隨父在台閣，習見故事。

translations.literal: At first, Qiong followed his father in the terrace and pavilion, and became familiar with precedents.
translations.idiomatic: Huang Qiong grew up in the ministries with his father and learned every precedent.

sentences.zh: 及後居職，達練官曹，爭議朝堂，莫能抗奪。

translations.literal: When afterward he held office, he was thoroughly versed in bureaus; in disputing at court, none could resist or seize from him.
translations.idiomatic: In office he mastered every desk; in court debate no one could overbear him.

sentences.zh: 時連有□異，瓊上疏順帝曰：「閒者以來，卦位錯謬，[一]寒燠相干，蒙氣數興，日闇月散。

translations.literal: At the time there were successive [lacuna] anomalies; Qiong submitted a memorial to Emperor Shun, saying: "In recent times the hexagram positions have been wrong, [1] cold and heat have interfered with each other, shrouded qi has repeatedly risen, the sun is dim and the moon scattered.
translations.idiomatic: Portents multiplied; Huang Qiong warned Emperor Shun: "Lately the cosmic order has slipped—[1] cold and heat clash, murky vapor veils the sky, sun and moon lose their light.

sentences.zh: [二]原之天意，殆不虛然。

translations.literal: [2] Tracing it in Heaven's mind, it is probably not empty thus.
translations.idiomatic: [2] Heaven does not warn without cause.

sentences.zh: 陛下宜開石室，案河洛，[三]外命史官，悉條上永建以前至漢初□異，與永建以後訖於今日，孰為多少。

translations.literal: Your Majesty ought to open the Stone Chamber, examine the River and Luo charts, [3] outwardly order the historiographers fully to list upward the [lacuna] anomalies from before Yongjian down to the beginning of Han, and from Yongjian down to today—which are more and which fewer.
translations.idiomatic: [3] Open the stone archive, consult the Hetu and Luoshu, and have historians list every portent from Han's founding through Yongjian and from Yongjian to the present—which cluster thicker?

sentences.zh: 又使近臣儒者參考政事，數見公卿，察問得失。

translations.literal: Also order your close ministers and Confucians to cross-examine government affairs, repeatedly meet dukes and ministers, and investigate gains and losses.
translations.idiomatic: Let your counselors and classicists review policy, meet ministers often, and weigh what works.

sentences.zh: 諸無功德者，宜皆斥黜。

translations.literal: As for those who lack merit and virtue, all ought to be cast out and dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: Dismiss every placeman without real merit.

sentences.zh: 臣前頗陳□眚，並薦光祿大夫樊英、太中大夫薛包及會稽賀純、廣漢楊厚，未蒙御省。

translations.literal: Your subject before repeatedly stated [lacuna] calamities, and recommended Grand Master of Splendid Carriages Fan Ying, Grand Counsellor Xue Bao, and He Chun of Kuaiji and Yang Hou of Guanghan—he has not received imperial examination.
translations.idiomatic: I have already listed portents and named Fan Ying, Xue Bao, He Chun, and Yang Hou—yet no edict followed.

sentences.zh: 伏見處士巴郡黃錯、漢陽任棠，年皆耆耋，有作者七人之志。

translations.literal: Your subject humbly sees the recluses Huang Cuo of Ba commandery and Ren Tang of Hanyang; their years are both hoary; they have the ambition of the seven who did the creating.
translations.idiomatic: I note recluses Huang Cuo of Ba and Ren Tang of Hanyang—both aged sages with the spirit of the "seven creators."

sentences.zh: [四]宜更見引致，助崇大化。

translations.literal: [4] It is fitting further to summon and bring them in, assisting and exalting great transformation.
translations.idiomatic: [4] Summon them to help spread your civilizing rule."

sentences.zh: 」於是有詔公車征錯等。

translations.literal: " Thereupon there was an edict for the public carriage to summon Cuo and others.
translations.idiomatic: An edict then called Huang Cuo and the others by imperial carriage.

sentences.zh: 注[一]易干鑿度曰：「求卦主歲術常以太歲為歲紀歲，七十六為一紀，二十紀為一蔀首。

translations.literal: Note [1] The Changes, Qian zao du, says: "The method of seeking the hexagram that governs the year always takes the Grand Year as the year's reckoning; seventy-six years make one ji, twenty ji make one bu head.
translations.idiomatic: Note [1]: the Yi qian zao du gives the math for the annual hexagram and the seventy-six-year cycle.

sentences.zh: 即置積蔀首歲數，加所入紀歲數，以三十二除之，不足除者以乾坤始數二卦而得一歲，未筭即主歲之卦也。」

translations.literal: Then set the accumulated bu head year count, add the years entered in the ji, divide by thirty-two; what does not suffice to divide, take the Qian and Kun starting numbers of two hexagrams to obtain one year; what is not yet reckoned is then the hexagram governing the year."
translations.idiomatic: The gloss explains how to derive the year hexagram from accumulated cycles.

sentences.zh: 注[二]蒙，陰闇也。

translations.literal: Note [2] Meng means yin darkness.
translations.idiomatic: Note [2]: "meng" means murky yin vapor.

sentences.zh: 散謂不精明。

translations.literal: San means not clear and bright.
translations.idiomatic: "San" means dim or unfocused light.

sentences.zh: 注[三]石室，藏書之府。

translations.literal: Note [3] The Stone Chamber is the repository where books are stored.
translations.idiomatic: Note [3]: the Stone Chamber was the imperial library.

sentences.zh: 河洛，圖書之文也。

translations.literal: He and Luo are the texts of charts and documents.
translations.idiomatic: Hetu and Luoshu are the river charts and Luo documents.

sentences.zh: 注[四]論語曰：「作者七人。

translations.literal: Note [4] The Analects says: "The seven who did the creating.
translations.idiomatic: Note [4]: the Analects speaks of "the seven who withdrew."

sentences.zh: 」注云：「謂伯夷、叔齊、虞仲、夷逸、朱張、柳下惠、少連。」

translations.literal: " The commentary says: "They mean Boyi, Shuqi, Yu Zhong, Yi Yi, Zhu Zhang, Liuxia Hui, and Shaolian."
translations.idiomatic: The gloss names Boyi, Shuqi, Yu Zhong, Yi Yi, Zhu Zhang, Liuxia Hui, and Shaolian.

sentences.zh: 三年，大旱，瓊復上疏曰：「昔魯僖遇旱，以六事自讓，躬節儉，閉女謁，於讒佞者十三人，誅稅民受貨者九人，[一]退捨南郊，天立大雨。

translations.literal: In the third year there was great drought; Qiong again submitted a memorial, saying: "In antiquity when Duke Xi of Lu met drought, by the six affairs he blamed himself; personally he was frugal, closed women's pleas, dismissed thirteen slanderous and flattering men, executed nine men who taxed the people and took bribes, [1] withdrew and lodged at the southern suburb, and Heaven immediately sent great rain.
translations.idiomatic: In the third year drought returned; Huang Qiong cited Duke Xi of Lu, who blamed himself on six counts, sealed the harem to influence, cashiered thirteen slanderers, executed nine extortionists, [1] moved his couch to the southern suburb, and rain fell at once.

sentences.zh: 今亦宜顧省政事，有所損闕，務存質儉，以易民聽。

translations.literal: Now also it is fitting to look back and examine government affairs, having places that are reduced and cut, striving to preserve substance and frugality, thereby to change what the people hear.
translations.idiomatic: You too should review policy, cut waste, live simply, and shift what the people hear.

sentences.zh: 尚方御府，息除煩費。

translations.literal: The imperial workshops and palace treasuries should rest and remove vexing expense.
translations.idiomatic: Idle the imperial workshops and palace treasuries of needless cost.

sentences.zh: 明□近臣，使遵法度，如有不移，示以好惡。

translations.literal: Make clear [lacuna] the close ministers, causing them to follow the statutes; if there are those who do not shift, show them good and evil.
translations.idiomatic: Warn your intimates to obey the law; if they will not change, show them reward and punishment.

sentences.zh: 數見公卿，引納儒士，訪以政化，使陳得失。

translations.literal: Repeatedly meet dukes and ministers, draw in and admit Confucians, consult them on government and transformation, causing them to state gains and losses.
translations.idiomatic: Meet ministers often, admit scholars, ask how to govern, and hear their verdict on policy.

sentences.zh: 又囚徒尚積，多致死亡，亦足以感傷和氣，招降□旱。

translations.literal: Moreover prisoners are still piled up, mostly leading to death—this also is sufficient to wound harmonious qi and summon downward [lacuna] drought.
translations.idiomatic: Jails are still crowded and inmates die in droves—enough to sour Heaven's breath and deepen drought.

sentences.zh: 若改敝從善，擇用嘉謀，則□消福至矣。

translations.literal: If you reform what is flawed and follow the good, choose and use fine plans, then [lacuna] will vanish and blessings will arrive."
translations.idiomatic: Mend what is broken, heed good counsel, and the portents will lift and blessings return."

sentences.zh: 」書奏，引見德陽殿，使中常侍以瓊奏書屬主者施行。

translations.literal: " When the document was submitted, he was led to audience in the Virtue-and-Clarity Hall; the palace attendant attached Qiong's memorial document to the responsible implementers for enactment.
translations.idiomatic: When the memorial went up, he was received in the Deyang Hall; a chief eunuch forwarded it to the bureaus for action.

sentences.zh: 注[一]春秋考異郵曰「僖公之時，雨澤不澍，比於九月，公大驚懼，率腢臣禱山川，以六過自讓，絀女謁，放下讒佞郭都*

translations.literal: Note [1] The Spring and Autumn, Kao yi you, says: "In Duke Xi's time, rain and moisture did not soak down; compared to September, the duke was greatly shocked and afraid; he led the multitude of ministers to pray to mountains and rivers; by the six faults he blamed himself, dismissed women's pleas, released the slanderous and flattering Guo Du*
translations.idiomatic: Note [1]: the Chunqiu kao yi you says Duke Xi of Lu, when rain failed until autumn, led ministers in prayer, confessed six faults, barred harem influence, and cashiered slanderers such as Guo Du

translations.literal: (and)
translations.idiomatic: (and)

sentences.zh: *等十三人，誅領人之吏受貨賂趙祝等九人。

translations.literal: *and others thirteen men, executed the clerks who led men and took bribes—Zhao Zhu and others nine men.
translations.idiomatic: —thirteen men in all—and executed nine corrupt tax clerks such as Zhao Zhu.

sentences.zh: 曰：『辜在寡人。

translations.literal: He said: 'The guilt is on me, the lonely one.
translations.idiomatic: He cried, "The fault is mine alone.

sentences.zh: 方今天旱，野無生稼，寡人當死，百姓何謗，請以身塞無狀』」也。

translations.literal: Now Heaven is dry and the wilds have no living grain; I, the lonely one, ought to die—what do the hundred surnames slander? I beg with my person to block my shapeless fault'"—thus.
translations.idiomatic: Now drought has burned the fields; I should die for it—why blame the people? Let me pay with my life for my failures.

sentences.zh: 自帝即位以後，不行籍田之禮。

translations.literal: From the time the Emperor ascended the throne afterward, he did not perform the ritual of the sacred field.
translations.idiomatic: Since his accession the emperor had not held the plowing rite on the sacred field.

sentences.zh: 瓊以國之大典不宜久廢，上疏奏曰：「自古聖帝哲王，莫不敬恭明祀，增致福祥，故必躬郊廟之禮，親籍田之勤，以先腢萌，率勸農功。

translations.literal: Qiong, because the great canons of the state ought not long to be abandoned, submitted a memorial, saying: "From antiquity sage emperors and wise kings have none not reverently honored bright sacrifice, thereby increasing and bringing blessing and auspiciousness; therefore they must personally perform the suburban and temple rites, personally labor at the sacred field, thereby to take the lead over the multitude of sprouts and lead and encourage agricultural work.
translations.idiomatic: Huang Qiong argued that the great state rites must not lapse: "Since antiquity no wise king has neglected sacrifice to win blessing; they always worshiped at the altars and plowed the sacred field to lead the farmers."

sentences.zh: 昔周宣王不籍千畝，□文公以為大譏，卒有姜戎之難，終損中興之名。

translations.literal: In antiquity King Xuan of Zhou did not plow the thousand mu; [lacuna] Duke Wen thought it great mockery; in the end there was the disaster of the Jiang Rong, and finally the name of mid-restoration was damaged.
translations.idiomatic: [1] When King Xuan skipped the thousand-mu rite, Duke Wen of Jin mocked him—then came the Jiang Rong raid and the stain on the "restoration."

sentences.zh: [一]竊見陛下遵稽古之鴻業，體虔肅以應天，順時奉元，懷柔百神，朝夕觸塵埃於道路，晝暮聆庶政以恤人。

translations.literal: [1] Your subject has humbly seen that Your Majesty follows the vast enterprise of examining antiquity, embodies reverent solemnity to answer Heaven, obeys the seasons and upholds the primordial, cherishes and softens the hundred spirits, morning and evening touches dust on the roads, day and night listens to the multitude of policies to pity the people.
translations.idiomatic: [1] Your Majesty revives antiquity, answers Heaven with reverence, tends the seasons and the gods, and toils day and night for the people—

sentences.zh: 雖詩詠成湯之不怠遑，書美文王之不暇食，誠不能加。

translations.literal: Although the Poetry praises accomplished Tang's not daring to be idle, and the Documents praises King Wen's having no leisure to eat, truly one cannot add to this.
translations.idiomatic: the Classic of Poetry praises Tang for never resting, the Documents praises King Wen for skipping meals—yet you surpass both.

sentences.zh: [二]今廟祀適闋，而祈谷絜齋之事，近在明日。

translations.literal: [2] Now temple sacrifice has just ended, while the prayer-for-grain pure fast is a matter of tomorrow.
translations.idiomatic: [2] The ancestral sacrifice has just closed, and the grain prayer begins tomorrow.

sentences.zh: 臣恐左右之心，不欲屢動聖躬，以為親耕之禮，可得而廢。

translations.literal: Your subject fears that the hearts of those to your left and right do not wish repeatedly to move the sacred person, thinking that the rite of personally plowing can be abandoned.
translations.idiomatic: I fear your attendants would spare you the plowing rite—thinking it may be dropped.

sentences.zh: 臣聞先王制典，籍田有日，司徒鹹戒，司空除□。

translations.literal: Your subject has heard that the former kings established the canons: the sacred field has its day; the Minister of Education all warns; the Minister of Works clears [lacuna].
translations.idiomatic: The ancient kings fixed a day for the plowing rite: the Minister of Education issued the summons and the Minister of Works readied the field.

sentences.zh: 先時五日，有協風之應，王即齋宮，饗醴載耒，誠重之也。

translations.literal: Five days before the time, there was a response of the harmonizing wind; the king entered the fast palace, feasted with sweet wine, loaded the plow—truly he took it as weighty.
translations.idiomatic: Five days ahead the warm "cooperating wind" blew; the king entered the fasting hall, poured the libation, and shouldered the plow—such was his solemnity.

sentences.zh: 自癸巳以來，仍西北風，甘澤不集，寒涼尚結。

translations.literal: From guisi onward, repeatedly there has been northwest wind; sweet moisture has not gathered; cold and cool still bind.
translations.idiomatic: Since guisi day after day the northwest wind has blown, no sweet rain has gathered, and cold lingers.

sentences.zh: [三]迎春東郊，既不躬親，先農之禮，所宜自勉，以逆和氣，以致時風。

translations.literal: [3] At the eastern suburb for welcoming spring, since you did not personally attend, for the rite of the former agriculture it is fitting personally to exert yourself, thereby to meet harmonious qi and bring timely wind.
translations.idiomatic: [3] You did not greet spring in person at the eastern suburb; for the First Farmer rite you should at least plow yourself to draw down mild weather.

sentences.zh: [四]易曰：『君子自強不息。

translations.literal: [4] The Changes says: 'The nobleman strengthens himself without ceasing.
translations.idiomatic: [4] The Book of Changes says, 'The superior man strives tirelessly.'

sentences.zh: 』斯其道也。

translations.literal: ' This is its Way.
translations.idiomatic: That is the path he means.'

sentences.zh: 」[五]書奏，帝從之。

translations.literal: "[5] When the document was submitted, the Emperor followed it.
translations.idiomatic: [5] The emperor accepted the memorial.'

sentences.zh: 注[一]國語曰，宣王即位，不籍千畝。

translations.literal: Note [1] The Discourses of the States says that when King Xuan of Zhou ascended the throne, he did not plow the thousand mu.
translations.idiomatic: Note one: the Guoyu says King Xuan of Zhou neglected the thousand-mu field.

sentences.zh: □文公諫曰：「夫人之大事在農，上帝之粢盛於是乎出，故稷為太官。

translations.literal: [lacuna] Duke Wen remonstrated, saying: "Among the great matters of men, agriculture is chief; from it come the pure offerings for High God; therefore Hou Ji was Grand Provisioner.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Wen of Jin warned him: 'A state's first business is farming—there the gods' grain comes from; hence Hou Ji was grand provisioner.'

sentences.zh: 古者太史順時覛土，農祥晨正日月，底於天廟。

translations.literal: In antiquity the Grand Historian observed the seasons and inspected the soil; the agricultural auspice at dawn corrected sun and moon; they reached the celestial temple.
translations.idiomatic: The grand historian watched the soil and stars until the farming omen aligned at dawn, then reported in the celestial temple.'

sentences.zh: 先時九日，太史告稷曰：『陽氣俱蒸，土膏其動。

translations.literal: Nine days before the time, the Grand Historian informed Hou Ji, saying: 'Yang qi has all steamed; the soil's fat is about to stir.'
translations.idiomatic: Nine days before plowing the historian told Hou Ji, 'Warmth has risen; the earth is ready to break.'

sentences.zh: 』稷以告王，王即齋宮，百官御事。

translations.literal: ' Hou Ji reported it to the king; the king entered the fast palace; the hundred offices attended to affairs.
translations.idiomatic: Hou Ji told the king, who entered the fast hall while all offices prepared.'

sentences.zh: 王耕一□，班三之，庶人終於千畝。

translations.literal: The king plowed one [lacuna], distributed thrice over it, and the common people finished at the thousand mu.
translations.idiomatic: The king opened one furrow, officials took three passes, and the people finished the thousand mu.'

sentences.zh: 」王弗聽，後師敗績於姜氏之戎。

translations.literal: " The king would not listen; afterward the army met disaster at the Jiang Rong of Jiang.
translations.idiomatic: The king refused—and later his army was crushed by the Jiang Rong.'

sentences.zh: □音扶發反。

translations.literal: [lacuna] is read fu fa fan.
translations.idiomatic: The damaged character is read fu fa fan.

sentences.zh: 注[二]詩商頌曰：「不僭不濫，不敢怠遑。

translations.literal: Note [2] The Shang odes of the Poetry say: "Not excessive, not wasteful, not daring to be idle or wander.
translations.idiomatic: Note two: the Shang hymns say, 'Neither lavish nor slack, never idle.'

sentences.zh: 」書曰「文王至於日中昃，不遑暇食」也。

translations.literal: " The Documents says "King Wen reached midday and sundown, without leisure to eat."
translations.idiomatic: The Documents praises King Wen for having no time to eat until noon and dusk.'

sentences.zh: 注[三]西北風曰不周風，亦曰厲風，見呂氏春秋也。

translations.literal: Note [3] The northwest wind is called the Bu Zhou wind; it is also called the fierce wind—seen in the Lüshi chunqiu.
translations.idiomatic: Note three: northwest wind is the Bu Zhou or "fierce" wind per the Lüshi chunqiu.

sentences.zh: 注[四]五經通義曰：「八風者，八卦之氣。

translations.literal: Note [4] The Comprehensive Meaning of the Five Classics says: "The eight winds are the qi of the eight trigrams.
translations.idiomatic: Note four: the Wujing tongyi identifies the eight winds with the eight trigrams.'

sentences.zh: 八風以時至，則陰陽變化之道成，萬物得以時育生之。」

translations.literal: When the eight winds arrive in season, then the way of yin-yang transformation is complete, and the myriad things are able according to the season to nurture and be born."
translations.idiomatic: When each wind comes on time, yin and yang work and creatures thrive.'

sentences.zh: 注[五]干卦象曰「天行健，君子以自強不息」也。

translations.literal: Note [5] The image statement of the Qian hexagram says "Heaven's movement is healthy; the nobleman thereby strengthens himself without ceasing."
translations.idiomatic: Note five: Qian's image reads, 'Heaven moves vigorously; the superior man strives without rest.'

sentences.zh: 頃之，遷尚書令。

translations.literal: Before long he was promoted Director of the Masters of Writing.
translations.idiomatic: Soon he became director of the Masters of Writing.

sentences.zh: 瓊以前左雄所上孝廉之選，專用儒學文吏，於取士之義，猶有所遺，乃奏增孝悌及能從政者為四科，事竟施行。

translations.literal: Qiong, because what former Zuo Xiong had submitted on the selection of filial and incorrupt used only Confucian learning and clerkly letters, as to the meaning of taking officers still had something left out, thereupon memorialized to add filial piety and fraternal duty and those able to handle government as four categories—the affair in the end was put into practice.
translations.idiomatic: Huang Qiong thought Zuo Xiong's filial-and-incorrupt rules over-weighted classicists and clerks, so he added filial piety, fraternal duty, and administrative talent as a fourth column—and the reform stuck.

sentences.zh: 又雄前議舉吏先試之於公府，又覆之於端門，後尚書張盛奏除此科。

translations.literal: Also Xiong's former proposal that recommended clerks first be tested in the public offices, again be reviewed at the Duan Gate—afterward Master of Writing Zhang Sheng memorialized to abolish this category.
translations.idiomatic: Later Zhang Sheng of the Masters of Writing struck out Zuo Xiong's double examination at the capital gate.

sentences.zh: 瓊復上言：「覆試之作，將以澄洗清濁，覆實虛濫，不宜改革。

translations.literal: Qiong again submitted words: "The work of review is intended to clarify and wash clear and turbid, to review substance and emptiness and excess—it is not fitting to reform and change it."
translations.idiomatic: Huang Qiong protested: 'The second review sorts the genuine from the hollow—it must not be scrapped.'

sentences.zh: 」帝乃止。

translations.literal: " The Emperor thereupon stopped.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor dropped the repeal.

sentences.zh: 出為魏郡太守，稍遷太常。

translations.literal: He went out as Grand Administrator of Wei commandery and was gradually promoted Minister of Ceremonies.
translations.idiomatic: He left the capital for Wei commandery, then rose to minister of ceremonies.

sentences.zh: 和平中，以選入侍講禁中。

translations.literal: In the Heping period, by selection he entered to lecture within the forbidden precincts.
translations.idiomatic: During Heping he was chosen to lecture inside the palace.

sentences.zh: 注[二]禮記明堂位曰「周公相武王以伐紂。

translations.literal: Note [2] The Record of Rites, "Bright Hall Positions," says "The Duke of Zhou assisted King Wu in attacking Zhou.
translations.idiomatic: Note two: the Mingtang wei says the Duke of Zhou aided King Wu against the last king of Shang.'

sentences.zh: 武王崩，成王幼弱，周公踐天子位，以理天下。

translations.literal: King Wu died; King Cheng was young and weak; the Duke of Zhou trod the Son of Heaven's position to order the realm under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: When Wu died and Cheng was a child, the duke acted as regent.'

sentences.zh: 七年，致政於成王。

translations.literal: In seven years he returned government to King Cheng.
translations.idiomatic: After seven years he handed power back to Cheng.'

sentences.zh: 成王以周公有勳勞於天下，是以封周公於曲阜，地方七百裡，革車千乘，命魯公世世祀周公以天子之禮樂」也。

translations.literal: King Cheng, because the Duke of Zhou had meritorious toil for the realm under Heaven, therefore enfeoffed the Duke of Zhou at Qufu, seven hundred li square, a thousand war chariots, and ordered the Duke of Lu for generations to sacrifice to the Duke of Zhou with the Son of Heaven's rites and music."
translations.idiomatic: Cheng rewarded him with Lu at seven hundred li, a thousand chariots, and rites equal to the Son of Heaven.'

sentences.zh: 注[三]高祖為泗上亭長，蕭何佐之，後拜何為相國，益封五千戶。

translations.literal: Note [3] Gaozu was pavilion chief on the Si; Xiao He assisted him; afterward he appointed He minister of state and increased his fief by five thousand households.
translations.idiomatic: Note three: Gaozu was a Si River pavilion chief; Xiao He rose with him to five thousand extra households as chancellor.'

sentences.zh: 霍光廢昌邑王，立宣帝，後益封光萬七千戶。

translations.literal: Huo Guang deposed the king of Changyi and established Emperor Xuan; afterward he increased Guang's fief by seventeen thousand households.
translations.idiomatic: Huo Guang deposed King Liu He and raised Xuan, then added seventeen thousand households to his fief.'

sentences.zh: 永興元年，遷司徒，轉太尉。

translations.literal: In the first year of Yongxing he was promoted Minister of Education and transferred to Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongxing 1 he became minister of education, then grand commandant.

sentences.zh: 梁冀前後所托辟召，一無所用。

translations.literal: Of Liang Ji's former requests for recommendations and summonses, not one was employed.
translations.idiomatic: He honored not a single nominee pushed by Liang Ji.

sentences.zh: 雖有善人而為冀所飾舉者，亦不加命。

translations.literal: Although there were good men whom Ji had decorated and recommended, he also did not add appointment.
